avoid: Do not promote or benchmark this candidate until it compiles and passes correctness.
Do not replace the dominant W @ X term with a custom full GEMM; use direct cuBLAS SGEMM.
Do not add handwritten rank-16 CUDA kernels before validating the simpler cuBLAS low-rank SGEMM sequence.
Do not explicitly materialize B.T as a transpose-copy.
Do not use opA=T/opB=T in SGEMM1 or SGEMM3 unless the row-major/column-major mapping is fully re-derived and tested.
Do not treat U as column-major [16,d], use m=16,n=d for U, or set U leading dimension to 16.
Do not hardcode hidden dimensions outside the runtime d handling.
evidence: Candidate did not compile, so correctness and benchmark results are unavailable.
NVCC failed at candidate.cu:4 with fatal error: ATen/cuda/CUDAGuard.h: No such file or directory.
Static review passed, but compile failure dominates all performance considerations.
The intended strategy, pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM without explicit B.T materialization and with beta=1 low-rank accumulation, is still the right direction but has not been validated.

recommended_actions: fall back to a simpler candidate

Repair the build first. Replace the unavailable include <ATen/cuda/CUDAGuard.h> with a header available in this environment, commonly <c10/cuda/CUDAGuard.h>, or remove the guard if inputs are guaranteed on the current device.
If using a device guard, use the c10 CUDA guard API consistently, for example c10::cuda::CUDAGuard guard(W.device()); with the appropriate include.
Keep the implementation as a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM path; do not fall back to ATen matmul/transpose/add or handwritten full GEMM kernels.

After compilation is fixed, validate the exact reference cuBLAS mapping: SGEMM1 opA=N/opB=N with A=X and B=W for the main W @ X term; SGEMM2 opA=N/opB=T with A=X and B=B, m=d, n=16, k=d, output U shape {d,16}, ldc=d; SGEMM3 opA=N/opB=N with A=U and B=A, m=d, n=d, k=16, beta=1 to accumulate into Y.
Ensure the temporary U is laid out with shape {d,16} and leading dimension d, not as {16,d}.
Ensure the low-rank path avoids explicit B.T materialization and accumulates directly into the existing Y using beta=1 in the third SGEMM.
Once it compiles, run correctness across all runtime d values in [3584, 4608] before considering benchmark results.
